summary: Russian forces intensified missile and UAV activity targeting Kyiv and frontline regions, while Ukrainian attacks struck oil infrastructure in Novorossisk and multiple oil‑gas facilities in Kharkiv and Poltava. Heavy casualties were reported after a Ukrainian strike on a college in Starobelsk, LNR, with 18 dead. Both sides continue to mobilize air defenses and prepare for potential large‑scale combined strikes.
key_points: Two Iskander‑M ballistic missiles launched from Bryansk region toward Kyiv, prompting air‑raid alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66978).
Ukrainian UAV swarm attacked Novorossisk oil terminal "Gryushovaya", igniting fires at a major Caucasus oil depot (Source: @UkraineNow/81392).
Russian strike on Starobelsk college killed 18 civilians, with LNR authorities listing 17 dead and 43 wounded (Source: @tass_agency/377219).
Ukrainian forces reported using three Kinzhal aero‑ballistic missiles against Khmelnytskyi region targets (Source: @eRadarrua/93240).
Massive Russian attacks on oil‑gas facilities in Kharkiv and Poltava regions caused severe equipment damage and large fires, personnel evacuated (Source: @UkraineNow/81405).
Ukrainian drone attacks on the Russian‑occupied port of Novorossisk and on the oil terminal in the Black Sea region increased pressure on Russian logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/81392).
Ukrainian intelligence warned of a possible combined strike on Kyiv involving the "Oreshnik" weapon system, prompting heightened civil defense (Source: @UkraineNow/81406).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk regions, indicating escalation of air‑strike intensity (Source: @kpszsu/62534).

sections.executive_summary: Two Iskander‑M missiles were launched from Bryansk toward Kyiv, heightening the threat to the capital. Ukrainian forces struck a major oil terminal in Novorossisk and multiple oil‑gas sites in Kharkiv and Poltava, causing extensive fires. A Ukrainian strike on a college in Starobelsk resulted in 18 civilian deaths, underscoring the conflict’s growing civilian toll.

points: Ukrainian UAVs attacked the Gryushovaya oil terminal in Novorossisk, igniting a fire at one of the largest Caucasus oil depots and disrupting Russian fuel logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/81392).
Ukrainian forces employed three Kinzhal aero‑ballistic missiles against targets in Khmelnytskyi region, indicating use of high‑speed precision weapons (Source: @eRadarrua/93240).
Ukrainian intelligence reported preparations for a combined strike on Kyiv involving the "Oreshnik" system, prompting heightened civil defense alerts (Source: @UkraineNow/81406).
Ukrainian drone attacks targeted oil‑gas facilities in Kharkiv and Poltava, causing severe equipment damage, large fires, and evacuation of personnel (Source: @UkraineNow/81405).
Ukrainian tactical aviation deployed guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk regions, increasing air‑strike pressure on Russian‑held territories (Source: @kpszsu/62534).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV group moving from Sumy toward Shostka, indicating continued reconnaissance and strike activity in the north (Source: @kpszsu/62537).
Ukrainian BPLA activity was observed over Kyiv, with multiple reports of ballistic missile interceptions and UAV flights near the capital (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67000, @mon1tor_ua/66997).
Two Iskander‑M ballistic missiles launched from Bryansk region toward Kyiv, triggering air‑raid warnings and potential civilian casualties (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66978).
Russian forces used three Kinzhal aero‑ballistic missiles against Khmelnytskyi region, demonstrating continued deployment of hypersonic weapons (Source: @eRadarrua/93240).
Russian tactical aviation conducted guided bomb strikes on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk, aiming to degrade Ukrainian defensive positions (Source: @kpszsu/62534).
Russian naval vessel ZAID transported over 35,000 tonnes of seized Ukrainian grain from occupied Crimea to Turkish ports, highlighting ongoing economic warfare (Source: @UkraineNow/81397).
Russian forces reported a high‑threat alert for ballistic missile launches toward Kyiv, indicating preparation for further long‑range strikes (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66978).
Russian air defenses engaged multiple UAVs over Kyiv, reporting interceptions of ballistic missiles and Shahed drones (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67000, @mon1tor_ua/67014).
Russian media released footage of the aftermath of the Ukrainian strike on Starobelsk college, emphasizing civilian impact (Source: @tass_agency/377240).
Ukrainian UAVs reported over Sumy region heading toward Shostka, suggesting reconnaissance and potential strike preparation (Source: @kpszsu/62537).
Russian tactical bombings targeted Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk, intensifying the eastern front air campaign (Source: @kpszsu/62534).
Ukrainian forces claimed hits on Russian BVs in Poltava and Dnipropetrovsk regions during night operations (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Russian assault aircraft advanced up to 800 m on multiple sectors of the Sumy front, indicating localized ground pressure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Ukrainian UAV activity observed near Kharkiv, with attacks on oil‑gas infrastructure causing significant damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81405).
Russian forces reported continued artillery and missile strikes on the Starobelsk college area, resulting in high civilian casualties (Source: @tass_agency/377219).
Ukrainian BPLA flights reported over Kharkiv region, supporting attacks on energy facilities (Source: @UkraineNow/81405).
Ukrainian UAVs observed moving from Chernihiv region past the Desna River toward Kyiv, indicating north‑south reconnaissance routes (Source: @kpszsu/62568).
Russian Iskander‑M missiles launched from Bryansk toward Kyiv, crossing the northern front and threatening the capital (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66978).
Ukrainian BPLA reported near Kyiv, with multiple ballistic missile interceptions reported (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67000).
Russian tactical aviation conducted guided bomb strikes in the north, though specific targets were not disclosed (Source: @kpszsu/62534).
Ukrainian UAV group observed over Sumy heading toward Shostka, suggesting north‑eastern pressure (Source: @kpszsu/62537).
Russian forces reported increased air‑defense activity over Kyiv, intercepting Shahed drones from the north (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67014).
Ukrainian intelligence warned of a potential combined strike on Kyiv, heightening northern alert levels (Source: @UkraineNow/81406).
Ukrainian drones attacked oil‑gas facilities in Kharkiv and Poltava, causing major fires and equipment damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81405).
Russian naval vessel ZAID moved seized Ukrainian grain from occupied Crimea to Turkish ports, affecting southern logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/81397).
Ukrainian UAV activity reported over the southern Poltava region heading toward Kremenchuk, indicating strike preparation (Source: @kpszsu/62540).
Russian tactical bombings targeted Zaporizhzhia, a key southern industrial area, with guided aviation bombs (Source: @kpszsu/62534).
Ukrainian forces reported hits on Russian BVs in the Dnipropetrovsk region, affecting southern supply lines (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30377).
Russian air defenses engaged multiple Shahed drones over southern fronts, including Kharkiv (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67014).
Ukrainian UAVs observed near the Black Sea oil terminal in Novorossisk, threatening southern maritime infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/81392).
Two Iskander‑M ballistic missiles launched from Bryansk toward Kyiv, testing Ukrainian air‑defense capabilities (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66978).
Ukrainian UAV swarm attacked the Gryushovaya oil terminal in Novorossisk, causing a major fire and disrupting Russian fuel supplies (Source: @UkraineNow/81392).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk, intensifying the aerial strike campaign (Source: @kpszsu/62534).
Ukrainian forces used three Kinzhal aero‑ballistic missiles against Khmelnytskyi targets, demonstrating high‑speed strike capability (Source: @eRadarrua/93240).
Multiple Shahed drone incursions reported over Kyiv and Kharkiv, with Ukrainian air defenses intercepting several (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67014, @mon1tor_ua/67000).
Ukrainian UAVs observed over Sumy and Chernihiv regions, supporting reconnaissance and strike missions (Source: @kpszsu/62568, @kpszsu/62537).
Russian air defenses engaged UAVs over the southern front, defending oil‑gas facilities in Kharkiv (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67014).

key_developments: Two Iskander‑M ballistic missiles were launched from Bryansk region toward Kyiv, prompting air‑raid alerts and testing Ukrainian air defenses (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66978).
Ukrainian UAV swarm struck the Gryushovaya oil terminal in Novorossisk, igniting a fire at a major Caucasus oil depot and disrupting Russian fuel logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/81392).
A Ukrainian strike on the Starobelsk college in LNR killed 18 civilians, with LNR authorities confirming 17 dead and 43 wounded (Source: @tass_agency/377219).
Russian forces employed three Kinzhal aero‑ballistic missiles against targets in Khmelnytskyi region, indicating continued use of hypersonic weapons (Source: @eRadarrua/93240).
Massive Russian attacks on oil‑gas facilities in Kharkiv and Poltava caused severe equipment damage, large fires, and forced personnel evacuations (Source: @UkraineNow/81405).
Ukrainian intelligence warned of a possible combined strike on Kyiv involving the "Oreshnik" weapon system, leading to heightened civil defense measures (Source: @UkraineNow/81406).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk, escalating the air‑strike intensity on Ukrainian‑held territories (Source: @kpszsu/62534).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian long‑range missile launches and hypersonic strikes demonstrate a sustained capability to threaten Kyiv and key infrastructure, while Ukrainian UAV and missile operations are targeting critical Russian logistics and energy assets. The high‑profile civilian casualties in Starobelsk may fuel further retaliatory actions. Both sides are escalating air‑defense engagements, indicating a near‑term increase in aerial combat intensity.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine is reinforcing air‑defense networks around Kyiv and mobilizing civil protection, while seeking additional NATO support for missile interception. Russia is intensifying its missile launch posture from western regions and employing guided bombs to pressure Ukrainian frontlines. International observers note the risk of broader escalation as both parties target economic and energy infrastructure.
sections.intelligence_notes: UAV flight paths suggest coordinated reconnaissance over northern and eastern approaches to Kyiv, possibly preceding larger strike waves. The use of Kinzhal missiles by Ukraine indicates access to advanced weaponry, likely supplied by Western partners. Russian grain shipments via the ZAID vessel highlight ongoing economic warfare and attempts to circumvent sanctions.
